mnemonics: Use the 'S.P.R.I.N.T.' mnemonic for the Agile Development Cycle: Speculate, Plan, Release, Iterate, Negotiate, Test.
keyPoints: Agile methodologies (e.g., Scrum, Kanban) focus on iterative development and customer feedback.
Risk Management involves identifying, analyzing, and mitigating potential project risks.
Version Control Systems (e.g., Git) are essential for tracking changes and enabling team collaboration.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on technical code quality while neglecting project documentation and requirements gathering.
Poor communication with stakeholders, leading to misaligned expectations and project scope creep.
Inadequate testing and quality assurance, resulting in buggy software releases.
